title: North Adams man convicted, sentenced for murdering his wife in 2022
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Luis Rosado, 53, was convicted of first-degree murder and sentenced to life without parole for killing his wife in 2022.
Rosado fatally stabbed Jillian Tatro, 38, in her North Adams apartment; she was stabbed 10 times and slashed 6 times.
Evidence including cell phone data led police to suspect Rosado; the DA highlighted the case's impact on the victim's family.

title: State officials release 4 submissions to immigration agent misconduct portal launched in March
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: State officials released four submissions from the ICE misconduct portal launched in March.
Incidents include unlawful detention at Logan Airport and ICE agent activity in Cohasset and Dennis.
Governor's office has not commented on the total submissions received since the portal's launch.

title: Weighing pros, cons of Celtics trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is considered as a potential trade target for the Boston Celtics to enhance their roster.
Celtics President Brad Stevens faces the challenge of trading key players like Jaylen Brown to acquire Antetokounmpo.
Antetokounmpo's age and recent injuries raise concerns about his long-term viability with the Celtics.

title: Harvard College faculty to vote on limiting number of As for students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Harvard faculty to vote on capping A grades at 20% plus four per class to address grade inflation.
The proposal follows a report indicating over 60% of Harvard College grades are currently As.
Critics argue the cap pits students against each other and creates grading ethical issues.

title: Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Julio Flores, a truck driver from Pawtucket, died from injuries days after a crash on I-95 South in North Attleborough.
The crash on May 5 involved Flores' Freightliner truck and a Volkswagen Jetta with four occupants, near mile marker eight.
State Police are investigating the crash that resulted in Flores being found away from the scene and hospitalized with severe injuries.

title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Sports Hub radio host Mike Felger shared a rumor about the Red Sox's interest in trading for Angels star Mike Trout.
Felger's source suggests the deal involves trading Jarren Duran and Brayan Bello for the 34-year-old, three-time MVP Trout.
Mike Trout, despite recent injuries, is batting .248 with 11 home runs in 41 games this season, creating interest from teams like the Red Sox.

title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Romeo Doubs adapts to New England Patriots offense under Josh McDaniels after four years with Green Bay Packers.
Doubs appreciates New England's quarterback Drake Maye for his energy and aims to build rapport with him.
The Patriots might pursue AJ Brown, which could affect Doubs' role despite his openness to multiple positions.

title: DA: Mass. man walked into police station, told officers he killed his father
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: David L. Boucher, 48, charged with first degree murder for allegedly killing his father, David A. Boucher.
Boucher reportedly confessed to North Adams police a week after allegedly stabbing his father to death.
Boucher arraigned and pleaded not guilty; held without bail; pretrial hearing set for June 12.

title: Worcester man, 62, killed by vehicle while crossing street, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: James McGuire, 62, was killed by a vehicle near 137 Hope Ave in Worcester on Sunday night.
Worcester police stated that the victim was crossing the street when he was struck shortly after 9 p.m.
The driver involved has not been identified, and no charges have been announced; the investigation is ongoing.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy suspended 6 games for slashing Sabres’ Zach Benson
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy suspended six games for slashing Zach Benson during Bruins' playoff loss to Sabres.
McAvoy hit with suspension following in-person hearing for his third NHL career suspension.
Bruins' coach Marco Sturm highlights McAvoy's need to control emotions despite standout season.
